review_text: This movie looks and is so good I don’t know why you haven’t seen it
Pixar at the height of its creative inspiration. A film that combines character development and thematic depth in a way few animated features manage to achieve.

I’m giving WALL·E a solid 3.5 stars. It’s weird I skipped this for so long as a Disney fan, because it’s honestly a beautiful, quiet little story. The animation carries so much emotion with barely any dialogue, and the comedy sneaks up on you. Underneath the cute robot romance is some pretty heavy social commentary about consumerism and the future, which makes it feel aimed more at older audiences than kids. It’s thoughtful, a bit sad, and surprisingly deep. Watch it for the message alone — it still feels important.
The characters are unique, and the animation and sound are GORGEOUS!! This is also living proof that Pixar can even make an environmental movie like nobody else can!
Best Pixar film of all time!

I watched this in theaters when it first came out and thoroughly enjoyed it. I recently re-watched it nearly 18 years after it's theatrical release and it is still fantastic. A very imaginative story with some top notch animation. It manages to tell a compelling story with a minimal amount of dialogue. Highly recommended!

One of Pixar’s greatest achievements. While I’m not one of those who would say that Wall-E is the studio’s best film, it’s undeniably a standout in Pixar’s catalog, and easily their most ambitious at that point in time.

This movie inched near perfect score with another reminder that us humans are littering way too much. I thought the characters were heartwarming just good. They did a great job with this movie alongside with Toy story 1-3.

Always love it. Childhood favorite. Makes me want to be a kid again. The characters may not talk a lot but hey, it's better than characters who keep dropping annoying jokes or swears all the time. Am I right? Everything about this movie is AMAZING. The animation to this day still looks INCREDIBLE for being 17 years old. The music is always so relaxing and soothing. Thomas Newman absolutely crushed it here. And, in the end, Wall-e has an important message about connection and environmental protection. Give it a watch, you won't regret it.

The animated movie everybody needs. Not just that, but I think that Pixar was a very important animation studio to exist & it was their very important movie to exist. I feel proud that earnt multiple awards. To start off, it is the animation, I never saw a worn down robot like Wall E look so realistic & rendered; I am unsure that I can say that with other movies from Pixar. This animation is just like a Blue Sky movie. The love story between Wall-E & Eve was not just a live story. They got to show each other their own worlds. Out of Wall Es collection of junk, he has an old boot with a plant in it. And Eve shows her where she lives, in an Axiom where humans helplessly got lazy staying there for a long time after Operation cleanup which was not succeeding. Wall E showed the boot with a plant & proved the Captain that there was hope on the planet. There were things to know more & it got better, better than the CEO of Buy n Large, Shelby Forthright. Talking about Buy n Large, it is a mega corporation that plays a part in this story. It has been economically greedy & never getting around to saving the environment, no wonder they went that way. It is what companies like Disney are kind of becoming now (yet "Wall E" came from Disney themselves). There is another movie involving this issue, but its more different, coming from Blue Sky/FOXs "Robots", which Disney also bought & Blue Sky had to get shut down due to the economic impact of COVID-19. Companies need to learn from this movie so thay life does not end up in disaster in the future. THEORY: Selby Forthright is either a holigram or originated from AI. Now, this movie could also mean the consequences of AI usage.

A wonderful and nostalgic experience since first watching this as a child and seeing it now as an adult really made me tear up knowing how good my childhood movie was one of my favorite movies still to this day. Well done Pixar!

I believe that this wonderful movie expresses deeply that AI has the potential to be our downfall in the future. WALL-E shows that the AI steering wheel tried to take over the spaceship because it thought that it was way smarter than us humans are. This movie shows a bit of real life in this fictional movie, in ways that AI is dominating society, even now. We use it when we generate images, we can create all kinds of things with AI, take ChatGPT for example. It's a great app to be sure, really helpful, but what if it becomes too smart because of us and tries to pull a WALL-E on us. I truly hope that AI doesn't become too smart, so that way AI domination isn't a possibility in our near future.
One of the best films ever, nothing more to say, one of THE BEST films ever

Sans dialogue, simplement avec les regards et les actions, ces deux personnages arrivent à nous toucher. La vision du futur de l’humanité fait froid dans le dos, et j’espère que nous n’arriverons jamais au stade légumier dans lequel sont ces humains. Ce film met également en avant un beau message pour nous rappeler que notre planète est importante et qu’il faut en prendre soin, en commençant pas les choses simples du quotidien, et surtout grâce à l’émerveillement de ce que la nature a à nous offrir.

An animation that is as beautiful as it is cautionary, this is absolute peak Pixar. A flawless, colorful, slice of life masterpiece that will pull on heartstrings no matter the age. Wall-E is an absolute timeless classic.

This movie is a cautionary tale unheeded, and despite its enduring charms, it's almost terrifying to re-watch in 2025. Beautiful film though. I could never recommend it eloquently enough, but I do recommend it wholeheartedly.
“WALL-E” doesn’t have much dialogue, but its stunning visuals and thought-provoking story prove that dialogue isn’t the key is a great movie.

Wall-E is one of those movies that will perfectly connect with a lot of people. The social commentary on consumerism, human ineptitude, and corporate power hits flawlessly here. We loved the romance between Wall-E and Eve, and just loved the adorable cuteness throughout the movie. The minimal dialog honestly helps this movie get across the emotions on display here. There is even tragedy here, and dark concepts that you wouldn't normally see in a Pixar movie. If you haven't watched this movie yet, you should.

It's genuinely one of my favorite movies of all time. The setting, the ambition, the sly critique of critics, the morals, the music. It's great.

Greetings from Lithuania. "WALL·E" (2008) is a great animated movie. Because its theme is so good, its story is so great and animation is so ingenues, it is safe to call this movie a true masterpiece. I've see this film many times during years, and i highly enjoyed it every single time. It is ingenues picture that was very risky one back in the day - it literally features no human for the majority of the movies first half, and only speaking parts are made by robots in a very robotic way. It is inspiring movie as well, and just a great science fiction story. The whole opening long sequence featuring WALL·E doin g his daily routine stuff in nothing short of a thing of beauty. And the music is perfectly done here. Overall, "WALL·E" is that one rare Pixar's masterpiece that is more enjoyable for grown ups then to the youngest ones. It has wonderful ideas and realizes them perfectly. This is one of the all time best animated movies.

"Io voglio vivere, non sopravvivere!" Questo è il messaggio di Wall E, ci è stato dato un grande dono, e noi uomini siamo così meschini da abbandonarlo. Un piccolo robot sarà capace di riaccendere la speranza in degli uomini abbandonati a sé stessi. Ascoltate il suo silenzio.

This movie really makes one to think, how crazy it is that this one of the other end of the world scenarios that is special. No big earthquakes, no alien invasions, no nuclear holocausts, but the fate of global warming and its effects of the environment. The stuff from 700 years is still there but it critical condition from weathering and corrosion. The humans have lost a lot of bone structure and have became fat from lack of exercise and staying in chairs forever. The plant is like a light in a dark room, like saving grace. These humans must have known that they were doing too much and they escaped. The orbit of the planet is full of satellites and I love that little Sputnik easter egg, which suggests that humanity had rebuilt the Sputnik satellite and launch it into orbit?

Many people call it the best Pixar animation ever. You know what? This is an understatement! It is the best animated movie in general! In German there exist an adage saying "A picture says more than a thousand words" and this movie, where the main characters can say 3 words, is the embodiment of the adage! The story is super emotional, the characters are really to empathize with and the animation really overshadow many modern movies!
